useCases: Technical portfolio calculations for 60/40 crypto allocation
Learning and teaching portfolio allocation concepts
explanation:
Personal finance and investment use universal mathematical principles: compound interest, time value of money, and risk-return optimization. This calculator applies standard formulas from the financial industry.

## How It's Calculated

The 60/40 crypto allocation divides your portfolio between traditional assets and cryptocurrency based on your input percentages.

This calculator applies the formula to your values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to any unusual figures.

## Typical Benchmarks by Time Horizon

| Time Horizon | S&P 500 | 10Y Bonds | Cash |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 year | Highly variable | 4–5% | 3–5% |
| 5 years | ~8% average | ~4% | Inflation |
| 10 years | ~9–10% | ~4% | Below inflation |
| 20+ years | ~10% nominal | ~5% | N/A |

## Investment Principles

- **Stick to your plan** during market downturns. Emotion is your worst advisor.
- **Diversify**: never concentrate in a single asset, sector, or country.
- **Keep costs low**: A 1% annual expense ratio eats 30% of your returns over 30 years.
- **Think long-term**: Equities work best over 10+ years, not 1 year.
- **Rebalance annually**: Once per year is typically optimal.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For significant financial decisions, consult a qualified professional. Values are current through 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.a: A traditional balanced allocation with 60% stocks and 40% bonds. Adding crypto creates a modern variation.

faq.q: How much cryptocurrency should I add to my portfolio?
faq.a: Conservative investors add 5–15% crypto for diversification. Your personal risk tolerance should guide your decision.
80% traditional + 20% crypto is popular among crypto-forward investors. Choose based on your risk tolerance and timeline.

faq.q: How often should I rebalance my portfolio?
faq.a: Most investors rebalance annually or quarterly. Sell winners, buy losers to maintain your target allocation.

faq.q: What are the risks of holding crypto in my portfolio?
faq.a: More crypto means higher volatility. Select an allocation that matches your risk tolerance and investment horizon.

faq.q: Does age affect the right allocation for me?
faq.a: Yes. Younger investors can tolerate more crypto volatility. As you approach retirement, reduce crypto for stability.

faq.q: What returns should I realistically expect?
faq.a: S&P 500 historical average: ~10% nominal annually (7% inflation-adjusted). Past performance doesn't guarantee future results.

faq.q: Is a 60/40 portfolio right for my goals?
faq.a: It depends on your objectives. Long-term retirement savings requires a different strategy than short-term speculation.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates required
